program.title: Ntsiniz Singing Transformation Journey — V6
program.description: Deep-research V6 curriculum that adds motor-learning progression, pressure ladders, health/load monitoring, richer style branches, ensemble/context transfer, stricter mastery gates, and professional-performance preparation while preserving compatibility with the 5-stage / 30-lesson / 180-drill structure.
program.north_star: from unsure beginner to healthy, expressive, repeatable performer across real singing contexts
program.core_loop: prepare -> hear -> sing -> reflect -> adapt -> transfer

levels.name: wooden_mic
levels.description: Cannot reliably match or hold a note

levels.name: beginner
levels.description: Can imitate some notes with low consistency

levels.name: developing
levels.description: Can match notes and simple patterns with support

levels.name: intermediate
levels.description: Can sing simple melodies with control

levels.name: advanced
levels.description: Can shape tone, phrasing, and dynamics intentionally

levels.name: pro_refinement
levels.description: Performance-ready singer seeking consistency and polish

drill_type_taxonomy.name: sustain_hold
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Hold one pitch steadily for a target duration

drill_type_taxonomy.name: pitch_slide
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Glide into or across pitches intentionally

drill_type_taxonomy.name: interval_jump
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Move accurately between two or more target notes

drill_type_taxonomy.name: phrase_sing
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Sing a phrase with legato and breath planning

drill_type_taxonomy.name: dynamic_control
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Control volume shape while maintaining pitch

drill_type_taxonomy.name: vowel_shape
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Maintain target vowel and resonance qualities

drill_type_taxonomy.name: vibrato_control
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Control straight tone and deliberate vibrato release

drill_type_taxonomy.name: performance_run
drill_type_taxonomy.description: Sing a longer line or excerpt with consistency

route_taxonomy.name: absolute_beginner
route_taxonomy.description: Starts at pitch awareness and first wins

route_taxonomy.name: weak_pitch
route_taxonomy.description: For learners who sing often but struggle with accuracy

route_taxonomy.name: confidence_builder
route_taxonomy.description: For shy learners who need safe repetition and reduced pressure

route_taxonomy.name: advanced_fast_track
route_taxonomy.description: Compressed route for already capable singers

style_branch_taxonomy.name: pop_rnb
style_branch_taxonomy.title: Pop / R&B
style_branch_taxonomy.description: Speech-informed connection, groove precision, clean melisma preparation, intimate mic-aware delivery.

focus_by_stage.S1: easy speech-connected onset, groove comfort, microphone-aware softness
focus_by_stage.S2: clean pitch center while keeping conversational ease
focus_by_stage.S3: vocal stylism, riff preparation, intimacy without collapse
focus_by_stage.S4: phrase pocket, consonant rhythm, emotional intimacy
focus_by_stage.S5: studio take repeatability and mic detail

style_branch_taxonomy.name: music_theater
style_branch_taxonomy.title: Music Theater
style_branch_taxonomy.description: Text-forward delivery, speech-to-song continuity, range-specific registration choices, durable repetition.

focus_by_stage.S1: body organization and easy speech-derived onset
focus_by_stage.S3: bright speech-derived color, text clarity, registration balance

style_branch_taxonomy.name: gospel_worship
style_branch_taxonomy.title: Gospel / Worship
style_branch_taxonomy.description: Sustainable resonance, legato phrase leadership, emotional intensity without oversinging.

focus_by_stage.S1: easy onset and comfort in prayerful/sustained patterns
focus_by_stage.S3: resonance warmth plus stylistic cry/sob options when healthy
focus_by_stage.S4: dynamic lift, leadership phrases, call-and-response transfer
focus_by_stage.S5: service pacing, mic awareness, emotional stamina

style_branch_taxonomy.name: choir_ensemble
style_branch_taxonomy.title: Choir / Ensemble
style_branch_taxonomy.description: Blend, tuning discipline, diction unity, line consistency, section-awareness transfer.

focus_by_stage.S1: unified setup, blend-aware vowels, healthy entry
focus_by_stage.S2: pitch center under harmonic context and section movement
focus_by_stage.S3: blend vs solo color choices, diction uniformity
focus_by_stage.S4: phrase alignment, ensemble timing, listening transfer
focus_by_stage.S5: rehearsal stamina, part responsibility, conducting-related vocal pacing

style_branch_taxonomy.name: singer_songwriter
style_branch_taxonomy.title: Singer-Songwriter / Indie
style_branch_taxonomy.description: Honest tone, text intimacy, storytelling, phrase pacing, dynamic restraint and build.

focus_by_stage.S1: comfortable authentic phonation and self-accompanied ease
focus_by_stage.S2: pitch navigation while maintaining honest tone
focus_by_stage.S3: stylistic color without losing lyric clarity
focus_by_stage.S4: song-story transfer, phrase ownership, identity alignment

performance_context_taxonomy.name: ensemble_rehearsal
performance_context_taxonomy.description: blend, diction, and section listening context

performance_context_taxonomy.name: worship_or_service
performance_context_taxonomy.description: sustained participation with emotional immediacy and variable acoustics

performance_context_taxonomy.name: studio_recording
performance_context_taxonomy.description: repeatable takes, mic awareness, detail control

motor_learning_taxonomy.name: blocked
motor_learning_taxonomy.description: same skill repeated with low contextual interference

motor_learning_taxonomy.name: serial
motor_learning_taxonomy.description: small ordered variations around the target skill

motor_learning_taxonomy.name: mental_practice
motor_learning_taxonomy.description: imagined rehearsal and cue recall without full vocal load

stages.title: Embodied Setup & Efficient Phonation Foundations
stages.learner_profile: Singer is building body organization, inhalation habits, easy onset, first pitch-center trust, and healthy self-monitoring.
goals: stabilize alignment, inhalation, and easy release before chasing sound
build first reliable pitch-center and short-hold wins
teach low-load recovery and reflection habits from day one
entry_criteria: new singer, confidence route, or any user missing stable onset / pitch-center basics
exit_criteria: can produce easy onset/release, short stable holds, and phrase-level first transfer without safety flags
research_basis: body alignment and inhalation precede phonation and should be built from the center outward
easy semi-occluded / efficient phonation work supports healthy voicing and carryover
early reflection prompts support independence rather than passive dependence on external feedback
stages.load_profile: low to moderate with frequent reset work and short distributed bouts
stages.repertoire_transfer_focus: single words, short speech-derived syllables, first short phrases in comfortable zone
stages.promotion_gate_summary: technical + retention + health gates required; pressure gate optional but preferred

stages.title: Pitch Navigation, Registration & Range Reliability
stages.learner_profile: Singer can start tones more reliably and is now learning to travel, land, and negotiate range/register without grabbing.
goals: improve interval prediction, landing, and return-to-center ability
build register-bridge strategy and dynamic control without drift
keep articulation from pulling pitch off center
entry_criteria: stable S1 outcomes or advanced route calibration indicating basic onset and sustain are already reliable
exit_criteria: can navigate up/down, bridge registers, and keep text/dynamics stable across pitch
research_basis: range work should start from comfortable areas and expand gradually, not from extremes first
motor-learning progression should move from blocked to more variable task conditions after stability emerges
vocal load must be paced while endurance and range are being expanded
stages.load_profile: moderate with planned recovery and deliberate range pacing
stages.repertoire_transfer_focus: motivic cells, two- to four-note patterns, short hooks, ascending/descending lines
stages.promotion_gate_summary: technical + transfer + health gates mandatory; retention gate required for fast-track

stages.title: Resonance, Articulation, Stylism & Text Delivery
stages.learner_profile: Singer has better control and now learns how resonance, vowels, diction, groove, and style choices shape meaning.
goals: develop tone choices rather than one generic color
coordinate style with healthy registration and diction clarity
introduce genre-specific stylism under moderate pressure
entry_criteria: S2 technical reliability or advanced route equivalent
exit_criteria: can make intentional tone/stylism choices with clear diction and healthy setup
research_basis: style demands differ by genre and should not be treated as one generic bucket
diction is a systematic pedagogical domain, not late cosmetic polish
speech-derived production is especially important for music theater and many CCM contexts
stages.load_profile: moderate; higher contextual complexity but carefully monitored vocal load
stages.repertoire_transfer_focus: hooks, short lyric phrases, genre cues, diction and groove transfer
stages.promotion_gate_summary: technical + style + transfer + health gates required

stages.title: Musical Communication, Repertoire & Context Transfer
stages.learner_profile: Singer can execute skills and now must communicate meaning across phrase, repertoire, ensemble, and identity/context demands.
goals: build phrase architecture, breath mapping, diction clarity, and emotional pacing
transfer skills into repertoire excerpts and real-use contexts
introduce ensemble/camera/context intelligence without losing efficiency
entry_criteria: S3 style and diction foundations established
exit_criteria: can communicate clearly through phrase and repertoire excerpts across at least one context transfer condition
research_basis: expressive musicality, diction, and interpretive agency are core pedagogical goals
repertoire selection and identity alignment can affect engagement and meaningful transfer
ensemble and context demands should be trained explicitly rather than assumed
stages.load_profile: moderate to moderate-high with context-specific recovery
stages.repertoire_transfer_focus: repertoire excerpts, ensemble blend, self-tape and camera awareness, identity-aligned song/story work
stages.promotion_gate_summary: technical + transfer + communication + health gates required

stages.title: Professional Performance Systems, Pressure & Sustainability
stages.learner_profile: Singer is capable enough to work on repeatability, mic craft, pressure management, pacing, recovery, and professional habits.
goals: build one-take reliability and context-specific pressure tolerance
train microphone, studio, audition, camera, and pacing skills
embed sustainable load and recovery habits for long-term performance
entry_criteria: S4 repertoire transfer established and no major health warnings
exit_criteria: can repeat a prepared performance under pressure with healthy pacing and clear self-coaching
research_basis: performance anxiety and pressure materially affect vocal output and should be trained, not ignored
high-load performance work requires workload pacing, distributed practice, and recovery days
marking, pacing, speaking voice care, and studio/audition habits are professional skills
stages.load_profile: periodized moderate-high with strict health checks and mandatory low-load recovery days
stages.repertoire_transfer_focus: full sections, audition cuts, self-tapes, mic/studio tasks, repeatable performance packages
stages.promotion_gate_summary: all gates required: technical, retention, transfer, style/communication, pressure, health, independence

lessons.title: Posture, Breathing & Kinesthetic Grounding
lessons.purpose: Build a repeatable physical setup and inhalation routine that prepares singing instead of reacting after the sound.
lesson_outcomes: organize posture without rigidity
inhale silently and efficiently
notice tension before phonation begins
describe one useful body cue in your own words
lessons.repertoire_bridge: Bridge posture, breathing & kinesthetic grounding into repertoire using short excerpts before full-song or full-scene pressure.
lessons.carryover_cue: keep the easy setup while the task gets busier

lessons.title: Capstone Benchmark: Repeatable Performance System
lessons.purpose: Demonstrate a repeatable, healthy, communicative performance package.
lesson_outcomes: deliver a repeatable capstone performance
meet technical, stylistic, and health criteria together
show one clear self-coaching strategy
finish with a sustainable plan for future work
lessons.repertoire_bridge: Bridge capstone benchmark: repeatable performance system into repertoire using short excerpts before full-song or full-scene pressure.
lessons.carryover_cue: keep the easy setup while the task gets busier

practice_structure.check_in: brief body + voice readiness rating before singing
practice_structure.priming: 1-2 low-load reset patterns tied to the lesson skill
practice_structure.acquisition_block: blocked reps of the core mechanic with one simple cue
practice_structure.variability_block: small changes in vowel, key, tempo, or text once the mechanic is stable
practice_structure.transfer_block: short phrase/context application relevant to the lesson

reflection_prompt.starter: What felt easiest? What got harder when the task changed?
reflection_prompt.advanced: Which cue changed the result fastest and what broke under transfer?

practice_structure.cool_down: required on LT3/LT4 lessons; optional but recommended otherwise

mastery_gate.technical: core mechanic passes on at least 2 distinct reps
mastery_gate.retention: one successful spaced revisit or next-day check
mastery_gate.health: no unresolved yellow/red flags at lesson end
mastery_gate.independence: user can name at least one cue or decision that improved the result

lessons.pressure_policy: explicit performance pressure ladder with recovery days
health_watchouts: fatigue not resolving with step-down work
lessons.identity_repertoire_hook: Allow at least one song/text/context option that feels meaningful or identity-congruent where possible.

drills.title: Posture, Breathing & Kinesthetic Grounding — Reset
drills.instructions: Reset the body/voice setup for posture, breathing & kinesthetic grounding using the easiest version of the task before adding pressure.
coach_cues: primary cue: organize posture without rigidity
expected_mistakes: holding the breath instead of letting tone ride airflow
correction_cues: try an easier onset or semi-occluded gesture
drills.pass_criteria: completion >= 1 rep AND no safety abort
drills.fail_criteria: score below threshold OR unresolved safety flag OR low-confidence cap trigger
safety_notes: stop if throat or jaw starts gripping
do not chase a note by pushing volume
return to comfortable range if strain appears
stop if you feel pain or escalating strain
step down load or pressure if completion collapses

return to comfortable range if strain appears
stop if you feel pain or escalating strain
step down load or pressure if completion collapses

drills.title: Capstone Benchmark — Reset

one usable take beats five reckless ones

reset with low-load work before another take
drills.fail_criteria: score below threshold OR unresolved safety flag OR low-confidence cap trigger

do not chase a note by pushing volume
return to comfortable range if strain appears
stop if you feel pain or escalating strain
step down load or pressure if completion collapses

criteria.technical: stage benchmark drills meet pass threshold
criteria.retention: one benchmark drill repeated successfully after delay or at session end
criteria.transfer: one phrase or context drill passes
criteria.style_or_communication: style/communication evidence required from S3 onward, optional but logged in S1-S2
criteria.pressure: pressure rung appropriate to stage completed
criteria.health: no red flags and acceptable yellow-flag recovery behavior

sections.name: technical
sections.description: isolated skill execution under stable support

sections.name: retention
sections.description: spaced revisit or repeated execution after delay

sections.name: transfer
sections.description: phrase/repertoire/context carryover

sections.name: style_or_communication
sections.description: style-appropriate tone, diction, timing, or expressive intent as relevant to the stage

sections.name: pressure
sections.description: single-take or reduced-retry rep under appropriate pressure level

sections.name: health
sections.description: no unresolved strain/fatigue and sound load decisions

sections.name: independence
sections.description: accurate self-report of what improved or failed

promotion_rules: promotion requires technical + transfer + health gates in all stages
retention gate required for any fast-track promotion
style_or_communication gate required from S3 onward
pressure gate may be one rung below target only on confidence routes if all other gates are strong
independence gate cannot block promotion alone but should block fast-track if absent
outcomes: advance to next stage
repeat current stage with targeted remediation bundle
advance with low-confidence note and mandatory revisit
step down one load tier or one pressure rung until health is stable

routes.title: First Voice Route
routes.description: Most runway, lowest pressure, highest cue density; designed for unsure beginners and nervous starters.

routes.title: Pitch & Coordination Repair Route
routes.description: For singers who already sing often but need center, landing, range, and articulation repair.

routes.title: Confidence & Anxiety-Sensitive Route
routes.description: Protects completion and psychological safety while still collecting real evidence.

routes.title: Artist Development Route
routes.description: Balanced route through coordination, style, transfer, and performance readiness for most users.

routes.title: Professional Calibration Route
routes.description: For already capable singers who need efficient calibration, branch-specific refinement, repeatability, and pro habits.
routes.load_profile: LT2/LT3 with selective LT4 and strict health checks

lesson_completion_logic: lesson completes when average of completed drills >= stage threshold
at least one transfer-oriented drill must pass in every lesson from S1_L06 onward
LT3/LT4 lessons additionally require no unresolved yellow/red health flags
unlock_logic: next lesson unlocks after current lesson completes and health gate is clear
spaced retention checks can be injected before advancing to a new stage
advanced fast-track may skip one lesson per stage only after retention and transfer evidence
style branch labs unlock only after preceding technique lesson is stable enough to support style load
mastery_logic: technical mastery = 3 consecutive passes OR 4 passes out of last 5 attempts
retention mastery = one successful repeat after a spaced revisit
transfer mastery = one passed phrase/style/context task using the lesson skill
health mastery = no unresolved strain/fatigue markers across the last 3 relevant sessions
independence mastery = singer can identify the cue, decision, or setup that improved results
stage exit requires technical + transfer + health evidence, and from S3 onward style/communication evidence
fairness_guards: do not score unvoiced, reference-playback, or recovery-state frames
use best valid window for beginner sustain/match work
cap penalties from low-confidence input before labeling chronic weakness
do not promote on one lucky pass or demote solely on one bad fatigue day
periodization_rules: no more than 2 medium/high-load days in a row
after LT4, next prescribed lesson defaults to LT1 or LT2
assessment week reduces extra style/performance branches except where assessment specifically demands them

diagnosis_profiles.coach_tip_template: You are usually landing low. Start a touch higher, then settle gently instead of lunging upward.
You often arrive high. Begin lower and let the center come to you before holding.
You are arriving too aggressively. Slide in slower and stop earlier.
Your pitch falls as you hold. Think steady airflow and a slightly brighter internal target.
The sound is wobbling. Make it smaller and more centered before adding intensity.
The system is not hearing enough clear voice. Use a speaking-level tone and finish the rep.
Focus on whether the pattern moves up or down before worrying about exact distance.
Finish imperfect reps. Completion builds confidence faster than restarting.

diagnosis_profiles.detection_rule: strain_flag OR repeated score drop after LT3 lessons
diagnosis_profiles.coach_tip_template: Your system may be tired. Drop intensity, reset coordination, and come back lighter.

help_mode_trigger_rules: activate help mode after 3 fails on same drill in 24h
activate help mode if rolling 5-attempt average < 55
activate help mode if low_voiced_ratio occurs twice in a row
activate recovery mode if fatigue_risk pattern appears after LT3 lesson

help_mode_adjustments.improvement_floor_rule: if score improves by >= 15 percent from previous attempt, final_score = max(final_score, previous_score + 5), cap 74
help_mode_adjustments.practice_mode_stepdown: move from random to serial or blocked when instability spikes
help_mode_adjustments.pressure_ladder_stepdown: drop one pressure rung immediately after panic/abort/strain
help_mode_adjustments.reflection_prompt_strengthen: ask explicit self-coaching question after repeat fail

remediation_bundles.name: confidence_completion_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: bridge task completed without strain flag

remediation_bundles.name: fatigue_management_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: no fatigue flag for 48h and stable score rebound

remediation_bundles.name: pressure_stepdown_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: 2 successful completions with lower pressure and restored confidence

remediation_bundles.name: transfer_repair_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: 1 passed transfer task and no health flags

remediation_bundles.name: style_integrity_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: style task passes with no strain flag

remediation_bundles.name: load_management_bundle
remediation_bundles.exit_rule: fatigue returns to baseline and completion recovers

plan: Day 1: setup + onset + short pitch match
Day 2: easy onset + short sustain + reflection
Day 3: comfort slide + short phrase transfer
Day 3: low-load reset and diction review

milestone_checkpoints.title: Repeatable Performance System
milestone_checkpoints.reward: unlock pro habit badge + capstone card

eligibility_rules: baseline overall high plus no active strain or instability flags
must still clear retention and transfer gates before skipping content
cannot skip body/breath/onset resets entirely; they become shortened calibration checkpoints
skip_logic: may skip one lesson within S1-S4 after benchmark and spaced retention confirm stability
S5 cannot be skipped; performance systems must still be trained explicitly

tiers.name: low
tiers.description: Skill acquisition / reset work with low vocal demand and high cue density.
tiers.recommended_recovery: 30-90 seconds between reps; one full rest day per week minimum

tiers.name: medium
tiers.description: Coordination and transfer work with moderate phrase length or interval demand.
tiers.recommended_recovery: 45-120 seconds between sets; alternate heavier and lighter days

tiers.name: high
tiers.description: Performance, stamina, repeated-take, or demanding style application work.
tiers.recommended_recovery: 60-180 seconds between sets; follow with cooldown or next-day low-load session

rules: do not schedule more than 2 LT3 sessions in a row
after any LT3 lesson, next prescribed lesson defaults to LT1 or LT2 unless assessment week
if strain_flag or fatigue_flag appears, drop one tier immediately and route to reset/cooldown bundle
beginners should spend most early weeks in LT1 and LT2

gate_types.name: technical
gate_types.description: Singer can execute the target skill with reliable accuracy and healthy coordination.

gate_types.name: retention
gate_types.description: Singer can repeat the skill after time has passed, not just immediately.

gate_types.name: transfer
gate_types.description: Singer can carry the skill into phrase, repertoire, or context.

gate_types.name: style_or_communication
gate_types.description: Singer can meet the stylistic/communicative demand relevant to the stage.

gate_types.name: pressure
gate_types.description: Singer can maintain enough organization under the stage-appropriate pressure rung.

gate_types.name: health
gate_types.description: Singer can complete the work without unresolved health warnings or poor pacing.

gate_types.name: independence
gate_types.description: Singer can identify a useful cue/decision and self-correct with less teacher dependence.

global_rules: promotion should not rely on one lucky pass
stage exit requires technical + transfer + health evidence in all stages
style_or_communication becomes mandatory from S3 onward
pressure is trained progressively, not assumed
independence gates are especially important for fast-track and capstone promotion

default_flow.goal: capture physical, mental, and vocal-readiness state before loading the voice
default_flow.coach_rule: use short self-report plus one diagnostic sound

default_flow.goal: restore efficient coordination with low-load gestures before challenge work
default_flow.coach_rule: prefer SOVT/easy onset/light siren resets when available

default_flow.goal: isolate the lesson mechanic with low contextual complexity

default_flow.coach_rule: shift from blocked to serial or random only after stable success

default_flow.goal: apply the skill in phrase, repertoire, ensemble, mic, or camera context
default_flow.coach_rule: keep transfer short before lengthening the phrase
ask what felt easiest, what changed, and what cue worked best

default_flow.goal: de-load after heavier work and teach professional recovery habits
default_flow.coach_rule: use shorter, easier reps or marking rather than abrupt stop after high-load work

notes: session design is meant to mirror elite coach lesson architecture, not flat drill stacking
check-in, reflection, and cool-down are required on medium/high-load days
short diagnostic samples before and after work provide retention and fatigue clues

S1: single sound with no score emphasis
single note with light visual reward
short guided hold with one retry
S3: style cue under mild tempo pressure
S4: lyric + phrase task with meaning cue
phrase performed after one listen only
global_rules: never increase pressure before the singer can execute the base skill technically
pressure ladders must step down immediately after strain, panic, or repeated completion collapse
confidence routes may complete a stage with one rung below the default pressure target if other gates are strong

assessment_rubric_dimensions.name: efficiency_health
assessment_rubric_dimensions.description: absence of strain flags, reasonable load tolerance, healthy recovery behavior

assessment_rubric_dimensions.name: stability_repeatability
assessment_rubric_dimensions.description: can reproduce the target without one-off luck

assessment_rubric_dimensions.name: transfer_application
assessment_rubric_dimensions.description: can carry the skill into phrase/repertoire/context

assessment_rubric_dimensions.name: stylism_communication
assessment_rubric_dimensions.description: style-appropriate tone, diction, timing, and emotional intent

assessment_rubric_dimensions.name: independence_self_coaching
assessment_rubric_dimensions.description: can describe what worked and select a useful next cue

yellow_flags: reduced top or bottom access compared with warmup
persistent dryness or urge to clear
red_flags: loss of phonation or sudden breathiness not explained by task
rapid swelling/fatigue sensation that does not reset after low-load work
inability to speak comfortably after session

actions.yellow: drop one load tier, shorten transfer block, switch to reset bundle, log fatigue note
actions.red: end singing work, mark session incomplete for health reasons, direct user to rest/recovery guidance and escalate if persistent

vocal_health_monitoring.speaking_voice_rule: speaking load counts toward singing load; marking, reduced intensity, and pacing are part of training, not signs of weakness

after_LT3_or_LT4: reduced speaking demand for 15-30 minutes where practical
after_mock_audition_or_self_tape: brief reflection while sensations are fresh
next session defaults to LT1 or LT2 unless no fatigue markers
